source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism §4.11 (Force between two parallel currents, definition of the ampere) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. NCERT consulted for the Indian real-world anchor + the SI ampere definition only. The atomic concept: two long parallel wires exert a magnetic force on each other. Wire 1's field at wire 2 is B₁ = μ₀I₁/2πd; wire 2 (current I₂ in that field) feels F = I₂ L × B₁, giving force-per-length F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/2πd. The result that traps students: PARALLEL currents ATTRACT and ANTIPARALLEL currents REPEL — the exact opposite of like/unlike electric charges. By Newton's third law the two wires feel equal-and-opposite forces. This relationship DEFINES the ampere (1 A is the current that, in two infinite parallel wires 1 m apart, gives F/L = 2×10⁻⁷ N/m). Deepest confusions targeted: (a) 'like currents repel, like charges do', (b) the force depends on only one of the currents, (c) reversing BOTH currents flips attraction (it does not). Conceptual-only ship (no mode_overrides). EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a).

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture the thick parallel busbars in a substation or the twin conductors feeding a big motor. When a huge fault current surges through them, the bars physically jerk toward or away from each other — that mechanical kick is exactly this F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/2πd force, which is why heavy busbars are clamped down.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture the SI definition of the ampere itself: take two infinitely long parallel wires one metre apart; the current that makes them push or pull on each other with a force of 2×10⁻⁷ newton per metre IS one ampere. This very force is how the unit of current was defined.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture the windings packed side by side inside a powerful electromagnet or transformer coil. Adjacent turns carry current in the same direction, so they attract — the coil squeezes itself together. Engineers must brace the windings against exactly this inter-current force.

formulas.field_of_wire1: B₁ = μ₀I₁/2πd — wire 1's magnetic field at the location of wire 2.
formulas.force_on_wire2: F = I₂ L × B₁ — wire 2 carries current I₂ through wire 1's field, so it feels a force (RHR gives the direction).
formulas.force_per_length: F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/2πd — force per unit length; symmetric in I₁ and I₂.
formulas.attract_repel: Parallel currents ATTRACT; antiparallel currents REPEL (opposite to like/unlike charges).
formulas.newtons_third_law: The two wires feel equal-and-opposite forces (action–reaction), even when I₁ ≠ I₂.
formulas.ampere_definition: 1 ampere: the current that makes two infinite parallel wires 1 m apart attract with F/L = 2×10⁻⁷ N/m.

constraints: Wires are long and straight; the long-wire field B₁ = μ₀I₁/2πd is used (end effects ignored).
B from one wire is evaluated AT the other wire's line; both wires lie in the same plane.
The force is mutual and equal-opposite (Newton's third law) regardless of whether I₁ = I₂.
Parallel (same-direction) currents attract; antiparallel (opposite) currents repel.
Reversing BOTH currents leaves the force unchanged (attraction stays attraction).
Steady currents only — no induced-EMF / time-varying effects (that is the next chapter).

aha_moment.statement: Parallel currents attract — the opposite of like charges.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: STATE_5 shows both wires' force arrows pointing toward each other; reversing one current (STATE_7) flips them to point apart (repel).

scene_composition.text: Two long parallel wires, both carrying current upward

Orange dots = current flowing.
Left wire = I₁, right wire = I₂.
Both currents point up (parallel).

Question:
Do these two wires push or pull
on each other? Or nothing?
Same setup as two heavy busbars side by side in a substation.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two long parallel wires. The orange dots show current flowing — both currents point straight up.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो लंबे parallel wires हैं। ये orange dots current का बहना दिखाते हैं — दोनों currents सीधे ऊपर की ओर हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు పొడవైన parallel wires ఉన్నాయి. ఈ orange dots current ప్రవహించడాన్ని చూపిస్తాయి — రెండు currents నేరుగా పైకి ఉన్నాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Left wire carries I-one, right wire carries I-two. They are not touching.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बायाँ wire I-one ले जाता है, दायाँ wire I-two ले जाता है। ये एक-दूसरे को छू नहीं रहे।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఎడమ wire I-one మోస్తుంది, కుడి wire I-two మోస్తుంది. ఇవి ఒకదానికొకటి తాకడం లేదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: The question. Do they push apart, pull together, or do nothing at all?
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब सवाल यह है। क्या ये एक-दूसरे को दूर धकेलते हैं, पास खींचते हैं, या कुछ भी नहीं करते?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ప్రశ్న ఇది. ఇవి ఒకదాన్నొకటి దూరంగా నెడతాయా, దగ్గరకు లాగుతాయా, లేదా ఏమీ చేయవా?

STATE_2.title: Wire 1 makes a field at wire 2

scene_composition.text: Wire 1's field reaches across to wire 2

Wire 1 is a current — it makes
circular field lines around itself.
At wire 2 the field is
B₁ = μ₀I₁/2πd.

The green arrow at wire 2
is B₁ — wire 1's field,
right where wire 2 sits.
Farther apart (bigger d) → weaker B₁ at wire 2.

tts_sentences.text_en: Forget wire 2 for a second. Wire 1 is a current, so it wraps the space around it in circular magnetic field lines.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक पल के लिए wire 2 को भूल जाइए। Wire 1 एक current है, इसलिए यह अपने चारों ओर के space को circular magnetic field lines से लपेट देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక్క క్షణం wire 2 ని మర్చిపోండి. Wire 1 ఒక current, కాబట్టి ఇది తన చుట్టూ ఉన్న space ని circular magnetic field lines తో చుట్టేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Those circles reach all the way across to wire 2. The strength there is B-one equals mu-zero I-one over two pi d.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ये circles पूरे रास्ते wire 2 तक पहुँचते हैं। वहाँ इसकी strength है B-one equals mu-zero I-one over two pi d।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ circles పూర్తిగా wire 2 వరకు చేరతాయి. అక్కడ దాని strength B-one equals mu-zero I-one over two pi d.

tts_sentences.text_en: The green arrow marks B-one right at wire 2's position. That is the field wire 2 is sitting in.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हरा arrow ठीक wire 2 की position पर B-one को दिखाता है। यही वह field है जिसमें wire 2 बैठा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆకుపచ్చ arrow సరిగ్గా wire 2 position వద్ద B-one ని చూపిస్తుంది. wire 2 ఉన్నది ఈ field లోనే.

scene_composition.text: A current in a field feels a force

Wire 2 carries I₂ through B₁.
F = I₂ L × B₁.
The right-hand rule sets
which way it is pushed.

Right-hand rule → the force on
wire 2 points toward wire 1.
Wire 2 is pulled inward.
F = I₂ L × B₁ — the same force-on-a-current-in-a-field you already know.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now bring wire 2 back. It carries current I-two, and it sits inside wire 1's field B-one.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब wire 2 को वापस लाइए। यह current I-two ले जाता है, और यह wire 1 के field B-one के अंदर बैठा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు wire 2 ని తిరిగి తీసుకురండి. ఇది current I-two మోస్తుంది, మరియు ఇది wire 1 యొక్క field B-one లోపల ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A current in a magnetic field always feels a force, F equals I-two L cross B-one. The right-hand rule gives its direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnetic field में रखा current हमेशा एक force महसूस करता है, F equals I-two L cross B-one। right-hand rule इसकी दिशा देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnetic field లో ఉన్న current ఎప్పుడూ ఒక force ని అనుభవిస్తుంది, F equals I-two L cross B-one. right-hand rule దీని దిశను ఇస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The right-hand rule gives a force on wire 2 pointing toward wire 1. Wire 2 is pulled inward.
tts_sentences.text_hi: right-hand rule से wire 2 पर एक force मिलता है जो wire 1 की ओर इशारा करता है। Wire 2 अंदर की ओर खिंचता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: right-hand rule ప్రకారం wire 2 పై wire 1 వైపు చూపే force వస్తుంది. Wire 2 లోపలికి లాగబడుతుంది.

scene_composition.text: Now wire 2's field pushes back on wire 1

Wire 2 also makes a field B₂.
Wire 1 carries I₁ through it.
F = I₁ L × B₂.
The same rule pushes wire 1 in.

Right-hand rule → the force on
wire 1 points toward wire 2.
Wire 1 is pulled inward too.
Symmetry: each wire sits in the other's field and is pushed toward it.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now flip the roles. Wire 2 is a current too, so it makes its own field B-two, and wire 1 sits right inside it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब roles बदलिए। Wire 2 भी एक current है, इसलिए यह अपना खुद का field B-two बनाता है, और wire 1 ठीक उसके अंदर बैठा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు roles మార్చండి. Wire 2 కూడా ఒక current, కాబట్టి ఇది తన సొంత field B-two ని తయారు చేస్తుంది, మరియు wire 1 సరిగ్గా దాని లోపల ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Wire 1 carries I-one through that field, so it feels a force, F equals I-one L cross B-two. The right-hand rule gives its direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Wire 1 उस field में से I-one ले जाता है, इसलिए यह एक force महसूस करता है, F equals I-one L cross B-two। right-hand rule इसकी दिशा देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Wire 1 ఆ field గుండా I-one ని మోస్తుంది, కాబట్టి ఇది ఒక force ని అనుభవిస్తుంది, F equals I-one L cross B-two. right-hand rule దీని దిశను ఇస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The right-hand rule gives a force on wire 1 pointing toward wire 2. Wire 1 is pulled inward too.
tts_sentences.text_hi: right-hand rule से wire 1 पर एक force मिलता है जो wire 2 की ओर इशारा करता है। Wire 1 भी अंदर की ओर खिंचता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: right-hand rule ప్రకారం wire 1 పై wire 2 వైపు చూపే force వస్తుంది. Wire 1 కూడా లోపలికి లాగబడుతుంది.

scene_composition.text: Parallel currents ATTRACT

By Newton's third law, wire 1
feels an equal force toward wire 2.
Both arrows point inward —
the wires pull together.

Careful! Like CHARGES repel.
But like (parallel) CURRENTS
ATTRACT. Opposite rule.
Two forces, equal and opposite, both inward — the wires move toward each other.

tts_sentences.text_en: Wire 2 is pulled toward wire 1. By Newton's third law, wire 1 feels an equal and opposite force — back toward wire 2.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Wire 2 wire 1 की ओर खिंचता है। Newton's third law के अनुसार, wire 1 एक बराबर और विपरीत force महसूस करता है — वापस wire 2 की ओर।
tts_sentences.text_te: Wire 2 wire 1 వైపు లాగబడుతుంది. Newton's third law ప్రకారం, wire 1 సమానమైన మరియు వ్యతిరేక force ని అనుభవిస్తుంది — తిరిగి wire 2 వైపు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Both forces point inward, so the two wires pull together. Parallel currents attract.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों forces अंदर की ओर इशारा करते हैं, इसलिए दोनों wires पास खिंचते हैं। Parallel currents attract करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు forces లోపలికి చూపుతాయి, కాబట్టి రెండు wires దగ్గరకు లాగబడతాయి. Parallel currents attract అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the trap. Like charges repel, but like currents — same direction — attract. It is the opposite rule.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इस जाल से सावधान रहिए। Like charges repel करते हैं, लेकिन like currents — एक ही direction — attract करते हैं। यह उल्टा rule है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ ట్రాప్ ని గమనించండి. Like charges repel అవుతాయి, కానీ like currents — ఒకే direction — attract అవుతాయి. ఇది వ్యతిరేక rule.

misconception_watch.belief: Parallel (same-direction) currents repel, the way like electric charges repel.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: It is the opposite for currents: parallel currents ATTRACT. Each wire sits in the other's field and is pushed toward it.

scene_composition.text: F/L = μ₀ I₁ I₂ / 2πd

Put it together:
B₁ = μ₀I₁/2πd, then F = I₂LB₁.
Force per metre:
F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/2πd.

Symmetric in I₁ and I₂ —
both currents matter equally.
Bigger currents or smaller d
→ stronger force.
The force depends on BOTH currents and the separation d.

tts_sentences.text_en: Put the two pieces together. B-one is mu-zero I-one over two pi d, and the force is I-two L times that field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब दोनों हिस्सों को जोड़िए। B-one है mu-zero I-one over two pi d, और force है I-two L times that field।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు రెండు భాగాలను కలపండి. B-one అంటే mu-zero I-one over two pi d, మరియు force అంటే I-two L times that field.

tts_sentences.text_en: Per metre of wire, the force is mu-zero I-one I-two over two pi d.
tts_sentences.text_hi: wire के प्रति metre, force है mu-zero I-one I-two over two pi d।
tts_sentences.text_te: wire యొక్క ప్రతి metre కి, force అంటే mu-zero I-one I-two over two pi d.

tts_sentences.text_en: Notice it is symmetric — both currents count equally. Stronger currents or a smaller gap mean a bigger force.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ध्यान दीजिए यह symmetric है — दोनों currents बराबर मायने रखते हैं। ज़्यादा currents या छोटा gap मतलब बड़ा force।
tts_sentences.text_te: గమనించండి ఇది symmetric — రెండు currents సమానంగా లెక్కకు వస్తాయి. ఎక్కువ currents లేదా చిన్న gap అంటే పెద్ద force.

misconception_watch.belief: The force depends on only one of the currents (the 'source' wire).
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/2πd is symmetric — it depends on BOTH currents equally. Each wire is source and receiver at once.

scene_composition.text: Antiparallel currents REPEL

Flip wire 2's current downward.
Now the currents are opposite.
The force on each wire
reverses — it points outward.

Same direction → attract.
Opposite direction → repel.
That is the whole rule.
Reversing BOTH currents would keep them attracting — only the relative direction matters.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now wire 2's current points the opposite way — downward. The two currents are antiparallel.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब wire 2 का current उल्टी दिशा में है — नीचे की ओर। दोनों currents antiparallel हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు wire 2 యొక్క current వ్యతిరేక దిశలో ఉంది — కిందికి. రెండు currents antiparallel.

tts_sentences.text_en: Both force arrows now point outward. The wires push apart. Antiparallel currents repel.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब दोनों force arrows बाहर की ओर इशारा करते हैं। दोनों wires दूर धकेलते हैं। Antiparallel currents repel करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు రెండు force arrows బయటికి చూపుతాయి. రెండు wires దూరంగా నెడతాయి. Antiparallel currents repel అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same direction attracts, opposite repels. Reverse both together and they still attract — only the relative direction matters.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक ही direction attract करती है, उल्टी repel करती है। दोनों को एक साथ उल्टा कर दें तब भी वे attract करेंगे — सिर्फ़ relative direction मायने रखती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒకే direction attract చేస్తుంది, వ్యతిరేకం repel చేస్తుంది. రెండింటినీ ఒకేసారి తిప్పేసినా అవి ఇంకా attract అవుతాయి — relative direction మాత్రమే ముఖ్యం.

misconception_watch.belief: Reversing both currents together flips attraction into repulsion.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Only the RELATIVE direction matters. Reverse both and they still attract; reverse just one and they repel.

scene_composition.text: This force defines the ampere

Take two infinite parallel wires
1 metre apart, each 1 ampere.
Then F/L = 2×10⁻⁷ N/m.
That is how 1 A was defined.

A force you can measure
fixes the unit of current —
the ampere is a base SI unit.
Substation busbars are clamped precisely because this force is real and large at high currents.

tts_sentences.text_en: This force is so fundamental it once defined the ampere itself.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह force इतना मौलिक है कि कभी इसने खुद ampere को परिभाषित किया था।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ force ఎంత ప్రాథమికమైనదంటే, ఒకప్పుడు ఇది ampere నే నిర్వచించింది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two infinite parallel wires, one metre apart, each carrying one ampere, attract with two times ten to the minus seven newtons per metre.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो infinite parallel wires, एक metre की दूरी पर, हर एक में one ampere, two times ten to the minus seven newtons per metre से attract करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు infinite parallel wires, ఒక metre దూరంలో, ఒక్కొక్కటి one ampere మోస్తూ, two times ten to the minus seven newtons per metre తో attract అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: A measurable force fixing the unit of current — that is why the ampere is a base SI unit.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक मापने योग्य force जो current की unit तय करता है — इसीलिए ampere एक base SI unit है।
tts_sentences.text_te: current యొక్క unit ని నిర్ధారించే ఒక కొలవగల force — అందుకే ampere ఒక base SI unit.

scene_composition.text: Explore: change I₁, I₂, and the direction

Drag I₁ and I₂ — the force
arrows grow with both.
Press the button to reverse I₂
and flip attract ↔ repel.

Challenge:
Make the force as large as you can.
Then flip to repulsion.
F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/2πd — same direction attracts, opposite repels.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Drag I-one and I-two and watch the force arrows respond.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी। I-one और I-two को खींचिए और देखिए force arrows कैसे बदलते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు. I-one మరియు I-two ని లాగండి, force arrows ఎలా స్పందిస్తాయో చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Both currents grow the force together. Press the button to reverse I-two.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों currents मिलकर force बढ़ाते हैं। I-two को उल्टा करने के लिए button दबाइए।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు currents కలిసి force ని పెంచుతాయి. I-two ని తిప్పడానికి button నొక్కండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same direction, they attract. Opposite direction, they repel. Try both.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक ही direction, वे attract करते हैं। उल्टी direction, वे repel करते हैं। दोनों आज़माइए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒకే direction, అవి attract అవుతాయి. వ్యతిరేక direction, అవి repel అవుతాయి. రెండూ ప్రయత్నించండి.

STATE_1.label: Two parallel wires, currents up, dots flowing
STATE_1.caption: Two long parallel wires. Both currents point up. Orange dots show the current flowing. No field or force shown yet.

STATE_2.label: Wire 1's B-field circles + B1 vector at wire 2
STATE_2.caption: Wire 1's circular field reaches wire 2. The green arrow is B1 = mu0 I1 / 2 pi d at wire 2's location.

STATE_3.label: Force on wire 2 toward wire 1 (RHR)
STATE_3.caption: Wire 2 carries I2 through B1, so F = I2 L x B1 points toward wire 1. Wire 2 is pulled inward. The right hand shows the rule: fingers along I, curl into B1, thumb gives F.

STATE_4.label: Force on wire 1 toward wire 2 (RHR mirror of S3)
STATE_4.caption: Wire 1 carries I1 through B2 (wire 2's field), so F = I1 L x B2 points toward wire 2. Wire 1 is pulled inward too. The right hand: fingers along I1, curl into B2, thumb gives F.

STATE_5.label: Both forces inward — parallel currents attract (AHA)
STATE_5.caption: Newton's third law: wire 1 feels an equal force toward wire 2. Both arrows point inward — the wires attract.

STATE_6.label: F/L = mu0 I1 I2 / 2 pi d (symmetric: both fields + both forces)
STATE_6.caption: Combine B1 and F: force per length F/L = mu0 I1 I2 / 2 pi d. Symmetric — both wires' fields and both forces shown together.

STATE_7.label: Antiparallel currents -> repel
STATE_7.caption: Wire 2's current now points DOWN (antiparallel). The force arrows point outward — antiparallel currents repel.

STATE_8.label: Defines the ampere — 1 A each, 1 m apart (distance marker)
STATE_8.caption: Two infinite parallel wires 1 m apart, each 1 A, attract with F/L = 2e-7 N/m. This defines the ampere.

STATE_9.label: Sandbox — I1, I2 sliders + reverse-I2 toggle
STATE_9.caption: Drag I1 and I2 to grow the force; press reverse-I2 to flip attract/repel. F/L = mu0 I1 I2 / 2 pi d.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered this concept when they can explain that one wire's magnetic field at the other wire is B = μ₀I/2πd, that the second wire (a current in that field) feels F = I L × B so the force per length between two parallel wires is F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/2πd, that this expression is symmetric in the two currents and the two wires feel equal-and-opposite forces (Newton's third law), that PARALLEL (same-direction) currents ATTRACT while ANTIPARALLEL (opposite) currents REPEL — the opposite of like/unlike electric charges — that only the relative current direction matters (reversing both leaves attraction unchanged), and that this measurable force is what historically defined the ampere (2×10⁻⁷ N/m for two wires 1 m apart at 1 A).

questions.stem: Two long parallel wires carry current in the SAME direction. They:

options.D: Twist but feel no net force

distractor_misconceptions.B: Applies the like-charges-repel rule to currents (it is reversed for currents)
distractor_misconceptions.C: Thinks parallel wires do not interact magnetically
distractor_misconceptions.D: Confuses the linear force with the torque on a loop

questions.parallel_form_stem: Two straight wires side by side carry current the same way. What do they do to each other?

questions.stem: Two parallel wires carry current in OPPOSITE directions. The force between them is:

options.D: Always stronger than for parallel currents

distractor_misconceptions.C: Thinks opposite currents cancel any force
distractor_misconceptions.D: Invents a magnitude difference that does not exist (same |F/L|)

questions.parallel_form_stem: Current runs up one wire and down a parallel wire. Do they attract or repel?

questions.stem: The force per unit length between two parallel wires is F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/2πd. If you double ONLY I₁, the force:

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks the force depends on only one current or neither
distractor_misconceptions.C: Doubles both currents instead of one

questions.tested_idea: F/L is linear in each current (symmetric in I₁, I₂)
questions.parallel_form_stem: In F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/2πd, the current in just one wire is doubled. By what factor does the force change?

questions.stem: Wire 1 carries 6 A and wire 2 carries 2 A. How do the forces they exert on each other compare?

options.A: Equal in magnitude and opposite in direction (Newton's third law)
options.B: Wire 1's force on wire 2 is three times larger
options.C: Wire 2 feels a force but wire 1 feels none
options.D: They are equal and in the same direction

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks the bigger current pushes harder (ignores Newton's third law)
distractor_misconceptions.C: Thinks only the 'source' wire exerts a force
distractor_misconceptions.D: Forgets the forces are oppositely directed

questions.tested_idea: The two wires feel equal-and-opposite forces even when I₁ ≠ I₂
questions.parallel_form_stem: One wire carries 8 A, the other 2 A. Compare the magnitudes and directions of the forces on the two wires.

questions.stem: Two parallel wires attract. You reverse the current in BOTH wires at the same time. They now:

options.D: Attract only if the currents are equal

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks reversing both flips the force (only relative direction matters)

questions.tested_idea: Only the relative current direction matters; reversing both keeps attraction
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two attracting parallel wires both have their currents reversed together. What happens to the force?

questions.stem: What does the force between two parallel currents historically define?

options.A: The ampere (the SI unit of current)

distractor_misconceptions.C: Confuses the defining force with the unit of magnetic field

questions.tested_idea: This force defines the ampere (2×10⁻⁷ N/m at 1 m, 1 A)
questions.parallel_form_stem: The 2×10⁻⁷ N/m force between two 1 m-apart wires at 1 A fixes which SI base unit?
